feature.name: Fighter Subclass: Gladiator (HB)
feature.text:
Fight for show and crowd
"The crowd roars for blood, and I give them a legend"
Theme: Inspired by the Arena fights that were held in ancient Rome.
Playstyle: Balanced between offense, defense, and showmanship.
Perhaps no figure from ancient Rome is as famous as the gladiator — a warrior of the arena that fought to the death against beasts, criminals, and other gladiators, for the entertainment of Roman society. Most Roman gladiators were de-facto slaves, and as such had no rights in Roman society and were scarcely considered people. For every epic story of a gladiator becoming a legend of the arena and winning fame and freedom, thousands of others died anonymously on the sand. They led violent, dangerous lives and were subject to the whims of their superiors. Some managed to overcome these circumstances and became celebrities, but they were a very small minority.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 33 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Arena Favorite (Gladiator (HB))
feature.text:
When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can add your Charisma modifier to the damage roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 33 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 7: Arena Tactician (Gladiator (HB))
feature.text:
When you use a Bonus Action to use the Second Wind feature, you can choose a creature within 10 feet of you. That creature takes damage equal to your Fighter level or is pushed 10 feet away (your choice).
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 33 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 10: Duelist's Edge (Gladiator (HB))
feature.text:
When you are in a melee against only one creature, you have Advantage on attack rolls against that creature, and that creature has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 33 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 15: Roar of the Crowd (Gladiator (HB))
feature.text:
When you score a critical hit, all Hostile creatures within 15 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your Charisma modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). Failure: Frightened condition until the end of your next turn.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 33 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 18: Champion of the Arena (Gladiator (HB))
feature.text:
You gain +1 to your AC and attack rolls when at least one Hostile creature is within 5 feet of you. Additionally, when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points, you can use your Reaction to regain hit points equal to your Fighter level.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 33 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Fighter Subclass: Hoplite (HB)
feature.text:
Gain strength from the group
"Alone, I am strong. Together, we are unbreakable."
Theme: Inspired by the ancient Greek phalanx warriors, masters of formation fighting and shield tactics.
Playstyle: Teamwork, shield tactics, and defensive formations.
Hoplites were citizen-soldiers of Ancient Greek city-states who were primarily armed with spears and shields. Hoplite soldiers used the phalanx formation to be effective in war with fewer soldiers. The formation discouraged the soldiers from acting alone, for this would compromise the formation and minimize its strengths. The hoplites were primarily represented by free citizens — propertied farmers and artisans — who were able to afford a linen or bronze armor suit and weapons. Though most hoplites were not professional soldiers and often lacked sufficient military training, hoplites made up the bulk of ancient Greek armies.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 31 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Shield Wall Master (Hoplite (HB))
feature.text:
While you are wielding a shield and are within 5 feet of at least one Ally who is not Incapacitated or Unconscious, you gain the following benefits:
• You and your ally gain a +2 bonus to AC.
• You have Advantage on Strength saving throws to resist being shoved or moved, or against the Prone condition.
• Hostile creatures have Disadvantage on melee attacks against creatures within 5 feet of you.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 31 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 7: Shield Push (Hoplite (HB))
feature.text:
When you take the Attack Action while wielding a shield, you can use a Bonus Action to shove a creature within 5 feet of you. The target must make a saving throw whose DC is 8 plus your Strength modifier plus your Proficiency Bonus. Failure: the target takes Bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 31 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 10: Defensive Cohesion (Hoplite (HB))
feature.text:
When one of your Allies within 10 feet of you is hit by an attack, you can use your Reaction to impose Disadvantage on the attack roll. If the attack still hits, the Ally gains Resistance to the damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 31 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 15: Resolute Defender (Hoplite (HB))
feature.text:
At the start of your turn, if you are adjacent to at least one Ally who is not Incapacitated or Unconscious, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Fighter level.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 31 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 18: Bulwark of War (Hoplite (HB))
feature.text:
As a Bonus Action, you can enter a defensive stance for 1 minute. While in this stance:
• Allies within 10 feet of you gain a +2 bonus to AC.
• When an enemy misses a melee attack against you or an ally within 10 feet of you, you can use your Reaction to move up to 10 feet without provoking Opportunity Attacks and make a melee weapon attack against the attacker.
When you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 31 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Fighter Subclass: Hussar (HB)
feature.text:
Enhance your powers through ypur faithful mount
"A Hussar never flees - Death rides behind me!"
Theme: Mounted warriors known for devastating charges.
Playstyle: Hussars use speed and precision to dominate the battlefield.
Hussars were members of a class of light cavalry originally from the Kingdom of Hungary during the 15th and 16th centuries. The title and distinctive dress of these horsemen were subsequently widely adopted by light cavalry regiments in European armies during the late 17th and 18th centuries. In battle, they were used in such light cavalry roles as harassing enemy skirmishers, overrunning artillery positions, and pursuing fleeing troops. In many countries, the hussars and bosniaks actually retained their original Asiatic uniforms. In the late 17th and 18th centuries, many Hungarian hussars sought employment in other Central and Western European countries and became the core of similar light cavalry formations created there. Following their example, hussar regiments were introduced into at least twelve European armies by 1800.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 34 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Winged Charge (Hussar (HB))
feature.text:
You have advantage on saving throws to avoid being dismounted, and your mount has advantage on Dexterity saving throws. When you are on a mount and you move at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and hit it with a melee weapon attack, the target takes an additional 1d8 damage and must make a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Strength modifier). Failure: Prone condition.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 34 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 7: Lancer's Precision (Hussar (HB))
feature.text:
When you make a melee weapon attack from a mount, you can add your Proficiency Bonus to the damage roll.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 34 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 10: Battlefield Momentum (Hussar (HB))
feature.text:
If you move at least 10 feet before making an attack, you can use your Bonus Action to either take the Dash Action or make one additional melee weapon attack.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 34 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 15: Rallying Cry (Hussar (HB))
feature.text:
When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points, you and your Allies within 30 feet of you gain Temporary Hit points equal to your Fighter level.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 34 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 18: Glory of the Charge (Hussar (HB))
feature.text:
When you use your Winged Charge feature, the additional damage becomes 2d8, and creatures with the Prone condition by this feature have also the Stunned condition until the end of your next turn.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 34 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Fighter Subclass: Landsknecht (HB)
feature.text:
Powerful veteran combatant
"A contract is my creed, but my blade is my master!"
Theme: Veteran warrior modeled by Renaissance German and Swiss mercenaries.
Playstyle: Offensive-focused with two-handed weapons.
The term Landsknecht is derived from Middle High German and Middle Low German. Knecht means "servant." A Landsknecht is, therefore, a servant of the land. In its original context, it is difficult to see how this word would do the soldiers justice. It does not conjure up images of deadly fighters. In 1486, the first units of Landsknechts were raised. These early units did not have the brightly colored clothing for which the Landsknechts became known. In fact, one of these units was named the Black Guard and served for many decades after its founding. Landsknecht troops were drawn mainly from poorer classes in society, although it was not uncommon for nobles to take up positions as officers.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 32 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Zweihander Master (Landsknecht (HB))
feature.text:
When you hit a creature with a melee weapon that has the Heavy property, you can deal half as much damage to another creature within 5 feet of the target. When wielding a melee weapon with the Heavy property, you can use your Reaction to add +2 to your AC against one melee attack that would hit you.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 32 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 7: Mercenary's Expertise (Landsknecht (HB))
feature.text:
When you make an attack with a Heavy weapon as part of the Attack Action, you can add your Proficiency Bonus to the damage roll.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 32 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 10: Imposing Presence (Landsknecht (HB))
feature.text:
You can use a Bonus Action to force each creature of your choice that can see or hear you within 10 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Strength modifier). Failure: Frightened condition until the end of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 32 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 15: Relentless Advance (Landsknecht (HB))
feature.text:
Once per turn, if you reduce a creature to 0 hit points, you can immediately move up to 10 feet without provoking Opportunity Attacks and make another melee weapon attack as part of the same Action.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 32 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 18: Master of Carnage (Landsknecht (HB))
feature.text:
Your melee weapon attacks with Heavy Weapons score a critical hit on a roll of 18-20. Additionally, when you score a critical hit, you can make one additional attack against the same target.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 32 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Fighter Subclass: Musketeer (HB)
feature.text:
Disciplined yet elegant warrior
"Never fear quarrels, but seek hazardous adventures." — The Three Musketeers, Alexandre Dumas
Theme: Inspired by the noble yet daring musketeers of Alexandre Dumas' novels, this subclass represents disciplined warriors who excel in both melee combat and ranged attacks. Musketeers blend agility, skill, and charm, making them formidable duelistcombatants and battlefield tacticians.
Playstyle: A versatile, swashbuckling fighter combining precision, mobility, and tactical prowess.
The Musketeers of the military household of the King of France (Mousquetaires de la maison militaire du roi de France), also known as the Musketeers of the Guard (French: Mousquetaires de la garde) or King's Musketeers (Mousquetaires du roi), were an elite fighting company of the military branch of the Maison du Roi, the royal household of the French monarchy. They were founded in 1622 when Louis XIII furnished a company of light cavalry (the carabins, created by Louis' father Henry IV) with muskets. The Musketeers fought in battles both on foot (infantry) and on horseback (cavalry). They formed the royal guard for the king while he was outside of the royal residences (within the royal residences, the king's guard was the Garde du corps and the Gardes suisses). The Musketeers of the Guard wore an early type of military uniform with a tabard (known as soubreveste), indicating that they "belonged" to the King.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 35 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 3: Elegant Duelist (Musketeer (HB))
feature.text:
When wielding a weapon with the Finesse property or a musket, you can add your Charisma modifier (minimum +1) to your damage rolls.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 35 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 7: Flash of Brilliance (Musketeer (HB))
feature.text:
When you or an Ally of yours within 30 feet of you make a Test, you can use your Reaction to grant a bonus equal to your Charisma modifier. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 35 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 10: Gallant Protector (Musketeer (HB))
feature.text:
When a creature within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to swap places with it, taking the hit instead. You reduce the damage you take from the attack by an amount equal to your level + your Charisma modifier. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 35 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 15: Master Fencer (Musketeer (HB))
feature.text:
Once on your turn, you can reroll a melee weapon attack that you miss. You must use the new roll.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 35 (Homebrew)

feature.name: Level 18: Musketeer's Resolve (Musketeer (HB))
feature.text:
You gain advantage on saving throws against the Frightened, Charmed, or Paralyzed condition. Additionally, as an Action, you can make one melee and one ranged attack against all creatures of your choice within your melee reach and your weapon's range. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
Source: Old World's Might - Subclasses from Old Europe p. 35 (Homebrew)
